heroHeadline: GSF Advance Force Operational at 545 as Kenya MSS Concludes; Gang Clashes Hit Airport, Fuel Prices Surge
heroSubtitle: The Gang Suppression Force's advance contingent — approximately 545 personnel (400 Chadian, 70 Salvadoran, 75 Guatemalan) — is conducting joint operations with the Haitian National Police as the Kenya-led MSS mission formally concludes with 803 Kenyan officers withdrawn. On April 20, rival gang factions (Chen Mechan vs. Duvivier/Pyè 6 groups, former Viv Ansanm allies now splintered) clashed in the Plaine du Cul-de-Sac, sending stray gunfire to Toussaint Louverture Airport and forcing Sunrise Airways to suspend flights for the day. A 30–40% fuel price hike in April 2026 — attributed to Strait of Hormuz geopolitical tensions — has driven up food, water, and transport costs, deepening the humanitarian crisis for the 6.4 million Haitians already needing assistance. On April 22, Haiti's Minister of Commerce advanced trade and investment talks with Brazil's ambassador, discussing a memorandum of understanding and industrial park investment. With the August 30 election date now in jeopardy following the April 8 indefinite postponement of voter registration, the GSF must accelerate territory recapture while 23 communes remain inaccessible. The 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan remains only ~20% funded ($172M of $880M needed).
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, Jamaica Gleaner, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, ICI Haiti, JURIST, International Rescue Committee, NPR, NBC News, Capital FM Kenya, Dominican Today, Security Council Report
